mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held at an elevated level relative to its trailing 6-month average signals restrictive monetary conditions that raise the discount rate on future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 in magnitude over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following high-FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly lower than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a tight-labor-market threshold, wage and cost pressures raise expected inflation, prompting markets to price further monetary tightening, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.0% (tight labor market threshold)
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following UNRATE readings below 4.0% are positive or statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two non-overlapping periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a high federal funds rate indicates resilient consumer demand absorbing tight monetary policy, a combination historically associated with positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days because it reduces recession risk pricing despite restrictive rates.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS-growth/high-FEDFUNDS episodes produce negative or null 20d forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term's marginal explanatory power over either series alone is not significant (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production reflects expanding real supply-side capacity and corporate earnings potential, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: The correlation between 6-month INDPRO growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not statistically distinguishable from zero (|r| < 0.05) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals deteriorating interest-rate-sensitive demand and forward-looking consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the market prices weaker aggregate demand.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 10% over the trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Following HOUST declines exceeding 10% over 3 months, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or effect size is not significant (p > 0.05) versus unconditional distribution.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth combined with a stable-to-falling unemployment rate confirms a durable expansion phase, jointly signaling improving corporate revenue and consumer spending capacity that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 0 while UNRATE is flat or declining
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=158286 thousand and UNRATE=3.9 at snapshot
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS-growth/stable-UNRATE episodes fail to produce 60-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional mean by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) across at least two economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is elevated and the federal funds rate is simultaneously high, markets anticipate continued or accelerated monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk premia rise in anticipation of policy action.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes of high CPI YoY combined with high FEDFUNDS produce non-negative average 5-day forward returns, or the interaction coefficient is not significant beyond individual series effects (p > 0.10) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in real retail sales reflects strengthening consumer spending, a leading indicator of corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between trailing 3-month RSAFS growth and forward 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.
